completion_evidence: The drill, hook hardware, measuring tools, and curtain area are visible and staged.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No wall position has yet been measured or marked.
recovery_action: Measure an appropriate height beside the curtain and mark the hook mounting point.

completion_evidence: The person inspects the curtain edge, wall area, and staged mounting tools.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No measurement or mounting mark is yet visible on the wall.
recovery_action: Measure and mark the selected hook location beside the curtain.

completion_evidence: The curtain is pulled aside and the hook is held against a candidate wall position.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The position is being judged by hand and no confirmed measurement or mark is visible.
recovery_action: Measure the candidate location and mark the mounting holes before drilling.

completion_evidence: The hook is repeatedly aligned against a selected position beside the curtain.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The mounting-hole locations are not visibly marked and the measuring tool is not being used.
recovery_action: Confirm the height and mark both mounting-hole locations.

completion_evidence: The hook is held at the selected location while a pencil marks its mounting-hole positions.
recovery_action: Set down the hook and prepare the drill and anchors for the marked locations.

completion_evidence: The mounting position has been marked and the hook is checked against those marks before being set down.
recovery_action: Prepare a suitable drill bit and make pilot holes at the confirmed marks.

completion_evidence: The selected hook position is measured against the curtain and wall and the tape is retracted after the check.
recovery_action: Use the confirmed marks to drill pilot holes and install any required anchors.

completion_evidence: The drill is used at the marked wall locations and two pilot-hole points are visible.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The holes are still being drilled and no anchors are installed by the end of the interval.
recovery_action: Finish and clear both pilot holes, then insert the matching wall anchors.

completion_evidence: The pilot holes are visible and the correct wall anchors are unpacked and selected.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The anchors remain in hand and are not yet seated in the pilot holes.
recovery_action: Insert and seat one anchor in each prepared pilot hole.

completion_evidence: Green wall anchors are being pressed into the drilled mounting holes.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: At least one anchor is still being seated and the prepared mounting points are not visibly finished.
recovery_action: Seat both anchors flush and confirm that neither is loose before mounting the hook.

completion_evidence: The hook is attached at both prepared points and its screws are being tightened with a screwdriver.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: Final tightening and an alignment or stability check are still in progress.
recovery_action: Finish tightening both screws, align the hook, and confirm that it does not move.

completion_evidence: The curtain is gathered into the mounted tie-back, the hook remains aligned, and the curtain stays held to the side.
recovery_action: Keep the installation as finished and recheck the screws later if the hook develops movement.

completion_evidence: The person enters the bathroom where the holder is located.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The holder has not yet been inspected and no replacement rod or tools are visible.
recovery_action: Locate and inspect the holder, then bring the compatible replacement rod and any needed tools.

completion_evidence: The holder is inspected and a screwdriver is applied to its right-side fastener.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The old rod remains installed and removal has only begun.
recovery_action: Loosen the necessary fastener and compress or release the old rod without damaging the wall mounts.

completion_evidence: The right end of the old rod is being loosened and pried away from its retaining point.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The old rod is still engaged between the holder supports.
recovery_action: Continue releasing the right end, then compress or free the opposite end and remove the rod.

completion_evidence: The person continues manipulating and checking the rod end and retaining hardware with the screwdriver.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The old rod is visibly still spanning both holder supports at the interval end.
recovery_action: Release one rod end fully and remove the old rod from between the supports.

completion_evidence: The holder was inspected and worked on, but its rod remains in place.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The person leaves the bathroom for a cluttered adjacent room before removing the old rod.
recovery_action: Return to the holder with the needed replacement and finish releasing the old rod.

completion_evidence: The task context and unfinished holder work remain known from the prior interval.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: Instead of returning to the holder, the person adjusts a wall thermostat throughout this interval.
recovery_action: Stop the unrelated thermostat adjustment and return to removing the old holder rod.

completion_evidence: The person leaves the thermostat and walks back into the bathroom containing the holder.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The person has not yet resumed manipulating the rod by the end of the interval.
recovery_action: Approach the holder and finish releasing and removing the old rod.

completion_evidence: The person is back at the holder and reaches for the screwdriver positioned above it.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The old rod is still installed and no end has yet been freed in this interval.
recovery_action: Use the screwdriver to release the rod end and remove the old rod.

completion_evidence: One rod end is pried free, the opposite end is released, and the old rod is fully held away from both wall supports.
recovery_action: Prepare the replacement rod and roll, then seat the new rod between the supports.

completion_evidence: The removed rod components are separated and a telescoping spring-rod assembly is examined and compressed by hand.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: The rod is not yet seated between the holder supports and no toilet-paper roll is loaded.
recovery_action: Load the roll if required, compress the replacement rod, and seat both ends in the wall supports.

completion_evidence: The spring rod is compressed, seated between both holder supports, and pushed and tugged to check retention.
incompletion_or_error_evidence: No toilet-paper roll is loaded, so centered fit and free roll rotation are not verified.
recovery_action: Load a roll and confirm that the rod stays seated while the roll turns freely.
